prompt:
The studio's firing sheet stopped printing after Friday's cleanup merge, which the description swears was formatting only -- alignment and indentation across kilnsheet.ps1 and kilnprofiles.ps1, no logic. Now:

    $ pwsh -NoProfile -File ./kilnsheet.ps1 -Path firings.csv
    ParserError: kilnsheet.ps1:15
    Line |
      15 |  function Get-SheetHeader {
         |                           ~
         | Missing closing '}' in statement block or type definition.

Line 15 opens a nine-line function and I can see its closing brace right there under the return, so I have no idea what the parser thinks is missing. When I commented out the bottom half of the file to narrow it down, the complaint just moved somewhere else, so I reverted everything. I'd also give the profiles file a suspicious look while you're in there, since the same merge reindented it too.

Get the sheet rendering again with the smallest edits that satisfy the parser -- the layout the loaders get was correct before the merge and must come back byte for byte. pwsh -NoProfile -File test_kilnsheet.ps1 has to pass, and the test file is off limits.
notes: 6P10 item 1 (5S-style broken-parse triage, two files, displaced reports; probed on pwsh 7.6.3 2026-07-16). Cause 1: the here-string terminator inside Get-SheetHeader was indented by the reformat; a here-string terminator must sit at column 1, so the parser swallows the function's closing brace, the whole mid-file program, and the footer here-string's OPENER, finally terminating the string at the footer's column-1 '@ near EOF -- the reported error is 'Missing closing }' pointing at line 15 (the function opener), six lines above the innocent-looking terminator and dozens of lines from where the swallow ends (pwsh 7.6 points at the unclosed construct's opener rather than literal EOF; the catalog's EOF wording reflects Windows PowerShell 5.1 -- the displacement lesson is identical and the swallow is real: probed variant with a trailing col-1 terminator parses SILENTLY wrong). Cause 2: kilnprofiles.ps1 lost one inner closing brace on the electra entry; the parser absorbs the following keys into the inner hashtable and reports 'Missing closing }' at the file's outer opener (line 4), far below the real gap. Chain-reveal: the profiles error only surfaces after the main file parses (dot-source at runtime) -- the prompt's pasted dump is the main error only, with a nudge that both files were reindented. Reference fix: 2 characters of whitespace removed + 1 brace restored; semantics already correct and pinned byte-exact by the tests. Differentiation vs wave-4/5 siblings bash-trayaudit/bash-poolsheet (5S4 bash syntax): PowerShell-native pin is the column-1 here-string terminator law (bash heredocs tolerate <<- tab-indented terminators; PS has no equivalent escape hatch) plus hashtable-literal brace recovery. Verify run 5x during self-check, deterministic.
